---
title: Quick Start
---

#### Access and getting Started
If your research project does not have a project space yet, [request it](cost_access.qmd#how-to-request-access) first. 

Once a project space is created a user with the Group Manager role can [invite colleagues](group_manager.qmd) and set their [access rights](group_manager.qmd#how-to-changing-the-role-of-a-user-in-a-group). 

Newly added users receive an [invitation](sram_invitations_eduid_activation.qmd), after accepting they can [login](logging_in.qmd) with their institutional account (VUnetId), with their primary e-mail address as username.

#### Working With Your Project Space
A project space is a data folder for storing and organising data during your project. Depending on the Operating System you are using, different methods can be used to [transfer data](data_access_instructions.qmd). 

Although you cannot directly access the data in Yoda with analysis tools, there are [several ways](analysing_data.qmd) to work with your data. 

You can easily [search](finding_restoring.qmd) for your files in Yoda using the built-in search tool.

#### Metadata and Dataset Preparation
Yoda enables you to [add metadata](workflow_metadata.qmd) to describe and contextualise your dataset. You can find more information in the [Metadata](metadata.qmd) section. 

#### Archiving and Publishing
Once your research is complete, you can [archive](vault_archive.qmd) and [publish](vault_publish.qmd) it, so you can reference it with a DOI.
